title: GitHub Actions CI: run verify.py on every push
current_state: No .github/workflows/ directory exists. scripts/verify.py is the only gate and depends entirely on being run locally before commits - a forgotten run ships regressions. Costly tests (real Claude/Groq/Supabase calls) are already excluded from verify via the COSTLY_TESTS set, so CI can run keyless with no secrets.
target_state: A ~25-line .github/workflows/verify.yml: on push and pull_request, checkout, setup Python (match local version), pip install -r requirements.txt, run python scripts/verify.py --quiet, fail the job on non-zero exit. Private repo still benefits: catches 'forgot to run verify' pushes.
migration_plan: 1. Write .github/workflows/verify.yml: actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, pip install -r requirements.txt (with pip cache), python scripts/verify.py --quiet.

2. Check requirements.txt for install-time landmines in CI (playwright browsers, audio libs like pyaudio/pyttsx3, win32-only packages). If any fail on ubuntu-latest, either mark them optional/skipped in the workflow install or run the workflow on windows-latest to mirror the dev box - pick whichever is fewer lines.
3. Confirm verify.py needs no .env to reach PASS (costly tests excluded; anything else that reads env must degrade gracefully - if it does not, that is a bug worth its own ticket).
4. Push on a branch, watch the Actions run go green (Ash gates the push).
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS locally as always.
risk_notes: The dependency install is the only real risk - requirements.txt serves a desktop agent (audio, browser automation, OCR). Fallback if install fights back: first stage runs syntax-check-only (verify's ast pass) which needs almost nothing installed; full test stage can come later. Do not add lint/type-check/coverage jobs - verify.py is the gate, CI just runs it.
